description_long: This is a tall, slender stone obelisk with a square cross-section, rising from a broad, tiered base. The base consists of two stacked square platforms, the lower one being larger and slightly weathered, while the upper one is more refined and supports the shaft. The shaft itself is plain, with sharp, clean edges and no visible inscriptions or ornamentation. The transition from the shaft to the tip is marked by a subtle, faceted collar, leading to a pointed, pyramidal tip. The entire structure is made of a uniform, light gray stone, giving it a monumental and dignified appearance. The surface appears matte and slightly rough, typical of carved stone exposed to the elements.
description: A tall, plain stone obelisk with a square, tiered base and a pointed tip, made of light gray stone. The structure is unadorned and has a matte, slightly rough surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the square, tiered base and the full height of the plain shaft leading to the pointed tip.
description_view_1: Side view with similar features as the front, highlighting the symmetry and the clean, sharp edges of the shaft and base.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, emphasizing the uniformity and lack of ornamentation on the shaft and base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the square base and plain shaft, with consistent design elements all around.
attribute_base_design: The base is composed of two stacked square platforms, the lower one being broader and slightly weathered, while the upper one is more refined and supports the shaft. Both platforms are made of the same light gray stone and have clean, sharp edges.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is a simple, pointed pyramid shape, smoothly transitioning from the shaft with a subtle faceted collar and no additional decoration.
